title: The Unmapped Summit Approach

features.name: the dissolving path
features.desc-on-touch: The path you followed to arrive here has already begun erasing itself. Behind you, the ridge settles and forgets. Ahead, paths branch that were not there a moment ago.

features.name: the summit-stone that recedes
features.desc-on-touch: A marker placed by someone who summited here, but the date inscribed shifts with the light. The higher you climb, the farther up the summit appears.

features.name: the cairn that remembers climbing
features.desc-on-touch: Stacked stones recording each attempt to reach the peak. But the stones have learned to rearrange themselves—a descent climbed backward, climbs recorded as falls.

features.name: the compass-rose that learned uncertainty
features.desc-on-touch: A compass carved into stone, but all the directions point upward now. North and South have become concepts of altitude. Direction has become vertical only.

features.name: the throat-where-grammar-fails
features.desc-on-touch: At the ridge's narrowest point, language becomes ambiguous. Every word here means both climb and descent. The throat is where the map admits it has run out of words.

features.name: the false-summit memorandum
features.desc-on-touch: Notes left by climbers who reached what they thought was the peak, only to learn the mountain continues upward in directions unmappable. Their handwriting grows confused halfway through.

inscription: the ridge climbs and climbs; the summit keeps ascending; at its throat, up becomes indistinguishable from down; the map here has learned to unmap itself

file.name: Summit_Approach_Expedition_Logs_1952_Onward.txt
file.note: Expedition records from every attempted ascent, but each climber reports the peak at a different altitude. The logs suggest the mountain is not fixed in space but moves vertically with the observer's

exits.label: back down to the ridge where descents still know their direction
into the high-country render where the diagnostic system itself climbs
toward the slope where paths have learned to flow like water
into the thin air where up and down have become meaningless
